researchNotes: Strong real evidence — 4 confirmed hits across 2025/2026, including a classic name-confusion trap (S.N. Banerjee vs. the actual first INC president, W.C. Bonnerjee — easy to mix up given the similar surnames). Expanded 2026-07 pass: re-scanned OCR and found one more genuine hit (2017_q1 page 37 Q172, image-verified: 72 delegates at the first INC session), then added standard high-yield INC facts via web research — the Ilbert Bill backdrop, Badruddin Tyabji's first-Muslim-president distinction, the Annie-Besant-vs-Sarojini-Naidu 'first woman president' trap (a genuinely easy mix-up), the Lahore session/Purna Swaraj/Ravi river flag-hoisting, J.B. Kripalani as president at independence, and the Home Rule League's two journals — rounding out session-by-session coverage beyond the original pass's TN-leaning facts.

reading.intro: Fifth topic of Unit III. Real exam evidence favors founder/president-name traps (similar-sounding names swapped) and TN-specific angles on the national movement (language of political meetings, women legislators) over generic INC session-by-session history.

qa.question: Real exam (trap): Who is considered the 'Father of the Indian National Congress', and who was its FIRST president — was it S.N. Banerjee?
qa.answer: A.O. Hume (a retired British civil servant) is considered the Father of the INC. The first president was W.C. Bonnerjee (Womesh Chunder Bonnerjee) — NOT S.N. Banerjee (Surendranath Banerjee), a different, similarly-named contemporary leader. This name mix-up is a real, confirmed exam trap.

qa.question: When and where was the INC founded?
Real exam: Why did Lokmanya Tilak replace the term 'Swaraj' with 'Home Rule'?
qa.answer: Because the British government found the term 'Swaraj' highly objectionable and considered it seditious, so Tilak rebranded the same demand using the less provocative term 'Home Rule' instead.

qa.question: What was the 'Surat Split' (1907), and what was the 'Lucknow Pact' (1916)?
qa.answer: Surat Split (1907): the INC split into Moderate and Extremist factions at its Surat session. Lucknow Pact (1916): the Moderates and Extremists reunited, AND the INC and the Muslim League agreed to jointly demand self-government from the British.

qa.question: Real exam: Which party started the 'Anti-Non-Cooperation League' in the Madras Presidency?
qa.answer: The Justice Party (opposing Congress's Non-Cooperation Movement in the Madras Presidency, reflecting the Justice Party's distinct, often Congress-opposed, political stance in the Tamil region).

qa.question: Real exam: True or false — INC held its meetings in Tamil as well (to attract common people), while the Justice Party held meetings only in English.
qa.answer: TRUE on both counts — INC used Tamil too, to reach ordinary people, while the Justice Party's meetings were conducted in English only.

qa.question: Real exam: In what year was Dr. Muthulakshmi Reddy elected Deputy Speaker of the (Madras) Legislature?
Real exam: How many delegates attended the INC's first session (1885, Bombay)?
What was the Ilbert Bill controversy (1883), and why does it matter for INC's origins?
qa.answer: Law Member C.P. Ilbert proposed a bill allowing Indian judges/magistrates to try British subjects in criminal cases. Fierce, often racially-charged opposition from the British/Anglo-Indian community forced the bill's dilution — but the controversy sharply galvanized Indian political consciousness just two years before the INC was founded (1885), making it an important immediate backdrop to INC's origins.

qa.question: Who presided over the INC's 3rd session (Madras, 1887), and what distinction did he hold?
qa.answer: Badruddin Tyabji — the first Muslim president of the Indian National Congress.

qa.question: Trap to watch: Who was the FIRST woman president of the INC, and who was the first INDIAN woman president — are they the same person?
qa.answer: No — Annie Besant (British-born) was the first woman president of the INC, at the 1917 Calcutta session. Sarojini Naidu was the first INDIAN woman president, at the 1925 Kanpur session — a distinct, later 'first'. Don't collapse the two into one fact.

qa.question: What happened at the INC's Lahore session (December 1929)?
qa.answer: Jawaharlal Nehru presided (a generational shift toward younger leadership). On 31 December 1929, the Congress adopted the 'Purna Swaraj' (Complete Independence) resolution, and Nehru hoisted the tricolour flag on the banks of the Ravi river at midnight. The Congress also fixed 26 January as 'Independence Day', to be observed annually from 1930 onward — the same date later chosen for the Constitution's commencement in 1950.

qa.question: Who was the President of the INC at the time of India's independence (1947)?
What was the Home Rule League movement (1916), and how does it connect Tilak and Annie Besant?
qa.answer: Two separate but allied Home Rule Leagues were launched in 1916 — Bal Gangadhar Tilak's in Maharashtra/Karnataka, and Annie Besant's in Madras — both demanding self-government (Home Rule) for India within the British Empire. Besant also published the journals 'New India' and 'Commonweal' to promote the movement, alongside Tilak's separate rebranding of 'Swaraj' as 'Home Rule' (already noted above).

questions.prompt: Who is considered the 'Father of the Indian National Congress'?
Who was the FIRST president of the Indian National Congress?
questions.explanation: W.C. Bonnerjee — not S.N. Banerjee, a common name-confusion trap.

questions.prompt: Why did Lokmanya Tilak rebrand 'Swaraj' as 'Home Rule'?
options: Home Rule was a Congress tradition

questions.prompt: Which party started the 'Anti-Non-Cooperation League' in the Madras Presidency?
Which statement is correct about political meetings in the Madras Presidency era?
options: INC held meetings only in English
Justice Party held meetings in Tamil to reach common people
INC held meetings in Tamil too, to attract common people
questions.explanation: INC used Tamil as well as English to reach ordinary people; the Justice Party's meetings were English-only.

questions.prompt: Dr. Muthulakshmi Reddy was elected Deputy Speaker of the Madras Legislature in which year?
The INC split into Moderate and Extremist factions at which 1907 session, known as the:
The Indian National Congress was founded in which year and city?
Real exam: How many delegates attended the INC's first session (1885, Bombay)?
The Ilbert Bill controversy, which sharply galvanized Indian political consciousness just before INC's founding, occurred in which year?
The INC's 3rd session (Madras, 1887) was presided over by Badruddin Tyabji, who was notable as:
options: The first Muslim president of the INC
The founder of the Muslim League
The first Congress president from Madras
questions.explanation: The first Muslim president of the INC.

questions.prompt: Who was the FIRST woman president of the Indian National Congress (1917, Calcutta session)?
questions.explanation: Annie Besant — not Sarojini Naidu, a common trap.

questions.prompt: Who was the first INDIAN woman president of the INC, at the 1925 Kanpur session?
questions.explanation: Sarojini Naidu — distinct from Annie Besant, the first woman president overall (1917).

questions.prompt: At the INC's Lahore session (December 1929), presided over by Nehru, the tricolour flag was hoisted on the banks of which river as the 'Purna Swaraj' resolution was adopted?
questions.explanation: The Ravi river, at Lahore, on the night of 31 December 1929.

questions.prompt: Who was the President of the Indian National Congress at the time of India's independence (1947)?
Annie Besant's 1916 Home Rule League (based in Madras) promoted its cause through which two journals?
questions.explanation: New India and Commonweal (Tilak's separate Home Rule League used 'Kesari'/'Mahratta', which are distractors here).
